name: Shorshe Ilish
description: The crown jewel of Bengali cuisine — Hilsa fish in a pungent mustard gravy. Nothing beats this on a rainy Sunday.

ingredients.name: Hilsa fish (Ilish)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Rohu or Katla from KR Market fish section works well

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Available at Nature's Basket, Namdhari's, or Amazon Fresh

steps: Soak black and yellow mustard seeds in water for 20 minutes. Blend into a smooth paste with 2 green chillies and a pinch of salt.
Marinate fish pieces with turmeric and salt for 10 minutes.
Heat mustard oil in a wide pan until it smokes slightly. Reduce heat.
Lightly fry fish pieces for 2 minutes each side. Remove and set aside.
In the same oil, add mustard paste and remaining green chillies. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add ½ cup water, bring to simmer, then slide fish back in.
Cover and cook on low heat for 8-10 minutes. Drizzle 1 tsp raw mustard oil before serving.
prepNote: Soak mustard seeds in water for 20 min before grinding into paste.

name: Kathi Roll
description: Kolkata's legendary street roll — flaky paratha wrapped around spiced chicken or paneer with onions and chutney. Perfect weekday dinner.

ingredients.name: Chicken breast
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Boneless chicken thigh works even better — more flavour

steps: Marinate chicken with chilli powder, cumin, garam masala, lemon juice and salt for 15 minutes.
Cook chicken in a hot pan with oil for 6-8 minutes until done. Set aside.
In the same pan, cook parathas. Before the last flip, pour a beaten egg on the paratha and let it set.
Place paratha egg-side up. Add chicken, sliced onions, a squeeze of lemon, and green chutney.
Roll tightly and wrap the bottom half in foil or paper. Serve immediately.
prepNote: Marinate chicken with spices and lemon for at least 15 min before cooking.

name: Chingri Malai Curry
description: Tiger prawns in silky coconut milk — a Bengali celebration dish that tastes like pure luxury.

ingredients.name: Tiger prawns
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Large Vannamei prawns from any supermarket fish counter

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Available at Nature's Basket or Namdhari's

steps: Marinate prawns with turmeric and salt for 10 minutes.
Heat mustard oil. Fry prawns lightly for 2 minutes. Remove.
In the same oil, add bay leaves and cardamom. Then add onion paste and cook until golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, and salt. Cook 3 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk and sugar. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add prawns back. Cook 5-6 minutes on low heat. Do not overcook.
Serve with steamed rice.
prepNote: Marinate prawns with turmeric and salt 10 min before cooking.

name: Aloo Posto
description: Crispy-soft potatoes cooked in poppy seed paste — the ultimate Bengali comfort food. Ready in 20 minutes.

ingredients.name: Poppy seeds (posto)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Available at most grocery stores in Bangalore; also on BigBasket

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Nature's Basket or Namdhari's stocks mustard oil

ingredients.name: Kalonji (nigella seeds)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Available at specialty stores; skip if unavailable

steps: Soak poppy seeds for 15 minutes. Grind with green chillies and a little water into a paste.
Heat mustard oil until it smokes. Add kalonji seeds.
Add potatoes and fry on medium heat for 8-10 minutes until golden.
Add poppy paste, salt, and ½ cup water. Mix well.
Cover and cook on low heat for 5-6 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Serve with steamed rice and dal.
prepNote: Soak poppy seeds in water for 15 min, then grind into paste before starting.

name: Luchi & Alur Torkari
description: Puffy deep-fried white flour puri with spiced potato curry — the ultimate Bengali Sunday breakfast.

ingredients.name: Panch phoron
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Mix equal parts cumin, nigella, fennel, fenugreek, and mustard seeds

steps: Mix maida with a pinch of salt, 1 tsp oil, and enough water to make a firm dough. Rest 20 minutes.
For torkari: heat oil, add panch phoron and dried red chillies.
Add boiled mashed potatoes, turmeric, salt and ½ cup water. Cook 5 minutes, mashing slightly.
Divide dough into small balls. Roll into small circles (puri-size).
Deep fry in hot oil until puffed and golden. Drain on paper.
Serve hot luchis with the torkari.

name: Sada Alur Torkari
description: A clean, niramish potato curry with panch phoron and mustard oil — no onion, no garlic. Pairs with luchi, roti, or as a light side with rice.

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Available at Nature's Basket, Namdhari's, or Amazon Fresh

ingredients.name: Panch phoron
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Mix equal parts cumin, nigella, fennel, fenugreek, and mustard seeds

steps: Boil or pressure cook potatoes until just tender. Peel and cut into rough chunks.
Heat mustard oil in a kadai until lightly smoking. Lower heat.
Add panch phoron and dried red chillies — let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add potato chunks and toss to coat in the tempered oil.
Add turmeric, salt, and green chillies. Mix well.
Add ½ cup water, cover, and cook on low for 5–7 minutes until the curry comes together.
Finish with a drizzle of raw mustard oil. Serve with luchi, roti, or alongside dal and rice.

name: Macher Jhol
description: Light, fragrant fish curry — the everyday Bengali dal equivalent. Comforting, healthy, and on the table in 30 minutes.

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Available at Nature's Basket or Namdhari's

steps: Marinate fish with turmeric and salt for 10 minutes.
Fry fish in hot mustard oil for 2-3 minutes each side. Remove.
In the same oil, add cumin seeds, then potato pieces. Fry 3 minutes.
Add tomato, turmeric, cumin powder, and salt. Cook until tomato softens.
Add 1.5 cups water, bring to boil, add green chillies.
Slide in fried fish. Cover and simmer 10 minutes.
Serve with steamed rice.
prepNote: Marinate fish with turmeric and salt 10 min before frying.

name: Mishti Doi
description: Bengali sweet yoghurt — thickened milk set with caramelised sugar. Make it the night before, serve chilled. Perfect Bangalore dessert.
steps: Reduce milk on medium heat to about 750ml, stirring frequently. This takes ~20 minutes.
In a separate pan, caramelise 3 tbsp sugar until golden brown. Add a splash of the hot milk to deglaze.
Mix caramel into reduced milk. Add remaining 1 tbsp sugar and cardamom. Cool to lukewarm (~40°C).
Whisk in the yoghurt starter. Pour into earthen pots (matki) or ramekins.
Set in a warm spot (or oven with just the light on) for 6-8 hours, then refrigerate.
Serve chilled — sweet, slightly tangy, slightly smoky from the caramel.

name: Panta Bhat
description: Overnight fermented rice with mustard oil, onion, and green chilli — the original probiotic breakfast. Refreshing in Bangalore's heat.

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Available at Nature's Basket or Namdhari's

ingredients.name: Dried mango powder or lemon juice
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Amchur powder widely available; fresh lemon is fine too

steps: Soak cooked rice in water overnight (or at least 6 hours). It will ferment slightly.
Drain excess water, keeping some — the rice should be soft and slightly sour.
Add mustard oil, salt, and amchur/lemon juice. Mix gently.
Serve topped with sliced onion, green chillies, and a wedge of lemon.
Best eaten on a hot Bangalore morning. Cool, tangy, deeply satisfying.
prepNote: Soak cooked rice in water overnight (minimum 6 hours) to ferment slightly.

name: Bangladeshi Egg Roll
description: The UK Bangladeshi takeaway staple — a spiced omelette roll with salad and chilli sauce. London's Brick Lane in your kitchen.
steps: Beat eggs with spices and a pinch of salt.
Toast chapati/paratha in a dry pan. Set aside.
Pour egg mix into pan. Before it sets fully, lay chapati on top.
Flip together so egg is on top of chapati. Slide onto plate.
Top with shredded lettuce, sliced onion, and drizzle of chilli sauce.
Roll tightly and enjoy the London-Bengali experience.

name: Chicken Rezala
description: A Mughal-influenced white Dhaka curry — aromatic, slightly tangy, subtle spicing. The pride of Old Dhaka's kitchens.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.amount: 3 large, thinly sliced and fried golden

ingredients.name: Kewra water
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Rose water works as substitute; available widely in Bangalore

steps: Marinate chicken in yoghurt, ginger-garlic paste, and white pepper for 30 minutes.
Fry onions in ghee until deep golden. Drain and make a paste with a little water.
In the same ghee, add cardamom. Add chicken and cook on high heat 5 minutes.
Add fried onion paste and soaked Kashmiri chillies. Cook 10 minutes.
Add 1 cup water. Cover and cook 20 minutes on low until chicken is tender.
Finish with kewra water. The gravy should be white-golden and fragrant.
Serve with naan or rice.
prepNote: Marinate chicken in yoghurt and white pepper for at least 30 min before cooking.

name: Bengali Fish Pie
description: A brilliant UK Bengali creation — the classic British fish pie infused with mustard, turmeric, and green chilli. Best of both worlds.

ingredients.name: White fish fillets (cod, haddock)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Basa or Sole fillets from major supermarkets in Bangalore

ingredients.name: Cheddar cheese
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Amul processed cheese or any grating cheese works

steps: Poach fish in turmeric-spiced milk for 8 minutes. Flake into chunks.
Make a white sauce with butter, flour, and the poaching milk. Stir in mustard paste and chillies.
Fold fish into the sauce. Pour into a baking dish.
Mash potatoes with butter and salt. Pipe or spread over fish mixture.
Top with grated cheese. Bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until golden.
Rest 5 minutes. Serve with a green salad.

name: Shingara
description: Bengali samosa — smaller, crispier, with a drier potato-cauliflower filling and the signature flaky crust. Tea-time essential.

ingredients.name: Panch phoron
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Mix equal parts cumin, nigella, fennel, fenugreek, and mustard seeds

steps: Make dough with maida, 2 tbsp oil, salt, and water. Keep firm. Rest 20 minutes.
Heat oil. Add panch phoron, then cauliflower and peas. Cook 5 minutes.
Add potatoes, salt, and a pinch of amchur. Mix, dry out moisture. Cool.
Roll dough into oval shapes. Cut in half. Form cones, fill with mixture, seal edges.
Deep fry on medium heat 8-10 minutes, turning frequently until golden and crisp.
Serve with green chutney and tamarind sauce.

name: Dimer Devil
description: Deviled eggs Kolkata-style — hard-boiled eggs coated in spiced minced meat or potato, then fried golden. A colonial-era Calcutta favourite.

ingredients.name: Chicken mince or potato
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Potato version is equally delicious and easier to find

steps: Cook mince with onion, ginger-garlic, garam masala and salt. Cool completely. (If using potato, boil and mash with same spices.)
Coat each egg half completely in the mince/potato mixture, forming an oval shape.
Dip in beaten egg, then roll in breadcrumbs.
Shallow fry until golden brown on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Serve with mustard or kasundi (Bengali mustard sauce) and green chutney.

name: NYC Bengali Fusion Bowl
description: A New York Bengali creation — turmeric rice bowl with spiced chicken, raita, and panch phoron-roasted veggies. Weekday power meal.

ingredients.name: Cooked basmati rice
ingredients.amount: 2 cups (with turmeric + mustard seeds cooked in)

ingredients.name: Panch phoron
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Mix equal parts cumin, fennel, and nigella seeds

ingredients.name: Kasundi (Bengali mustard sauce)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Mix 1 tbsp mustard paste + 1 tsp honey + lemon juice

steps: Season chicken with turmeric, chilli powder, garam masala, salt. Grill or pan-sear 12-15 minutes. Slice.
Toss vegetables with panch phoron, oil, and salt. Roast at 200°C or stir-fry 8 minutes.
Make simple raita: yoghurt + roasted cumin + salt + chopped coriander.
Assemble bowl: turmeric rice base, sliced chicken, roasted veggies.
Drizzle kasundi over the top. Add raita on the side.
Weekday lunch sorted in 25 minutes.

name: Nolen Gur Kheer
description: Bengali rice pudding made with date palm jaggery — smoky, caramel-sweet, deeply nostalgic. A winter Kolkata classic.

ingredients.name: Short-grain rice (gobindobhog)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Short-grain or regular white rice works well. Gobindobhog available on BigBasket

ingredients.name: Nolen gur (date palm jaggery)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Regular jaggery (gur) works; add ½ tsp caramel essence for the smoky note. Nolen gur sometimes on BigBasket

steps: Bring milk to boil with bay leaf. Add washed rice.
Simmer on low-medium heat, stirring frequently, for 25-30 minutes until milk thickens and rice is fully cooked.
Remove bay leaf. Add cardamom.
Take off heat, let cool slightly (important — hot milk will break the jaggery's flavour).
Stir in grated nolen gur. It will melt into the kheer, turning it a warm caramel colour.
Serve warm or chilled. The flavour deepens overnight in the fridge.

name: Doi Maach
description: Fish cooked in a yoghurt-based sauce — creamy, mildly spiced, and elegantly simple. A lighter alternative to mustard fish.

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Available at Nature's Basket or Namdhari's

steps: Marinate fish with turmeric and salt. Fry lightly in mustard oil. Set aside.
Fry onions until golden in the same oil. Add ginger paste, cook 2 minutes.
Lower heat completely. Add whisked yoghurt gradually, stirring constantly to prevent curdling.
Add sugar, green chillies, ½ cup water. Bring to gentle simmer.
Add fried fish. Cook 8-10 minutes covered on low heat.
Serve with steamed rice.
prepNote: Marinate fish with turmeric and salt 10 min ahead; it should look golden before hitting the pan.

name: Dim Paurutu
description: Bengali French toast — bread dipped in spiced egg batter and pan-fried. The 10-minute weekday breakfast Bengalis grow up on.
steps: Beat eggs with chopped onion, green chilli, salt, and pepper.
Dip each bread slice into the egg mixture, coating both sides.
Heat butter in a pan. Pan-fry bread for 2 minutes per side until golden.
Serve hot with ketchup or a dab of Kasundi.

name: Mishti Doi Toast
description: Toast topped with mishti doi, banana slices, and a drizzle of nolen gur — a 5-minute Bengali twist on breakfast toast.

ingredients.name: Mishti doi
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Sweetened Greek yoghurt with a pinch of jaggery

ingredients.name: Nolen gur or jaggery syrup
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Regular jaggery melted with a drop of water

steps: Toast the bread slices.
Spread mishti doi generously on each slice.
Top with banana slices.
Drizzle nolen gur and eat immediately.

name: Nolen Gur Overnight Oats
description: Prep-at-night, grab-in-the-morning overnight oats with nolen gur, banana, and almonds. The Bangalore Bengali's commute breakfast.

ingredients.name: Nolen gur
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Any liquid jaggery or 1 tbsp honey

steps: In a jar, combine oats, milk, nolen gur, mashed banana, and cardamom.
Stir well, seal, and refrigerate overnight (minimum 4 hours).
In the morning, top with chopped almonds and a little extra gur if desired.
Eat cold straight from the jar — grab and go.
prepNote: Assemble jar and refrigerate at least 4 hours (or overnight) before eating.

name: Jhuri Dim (Bengali Scrambled Eggs)
description: Bengali-style scrambled eggs with onions, green chilli, and a hint of turmeric — far more flavourful than the Western version.

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Any cooking oil works, but mustard oil gives authentic flavour

steps: Heat mustard oil. Fry onion and green chilli 2 minutes.
Add tomato, turmeric, salt. Cook 2 minutes.
Pour in beaten eggs. Scramble gently on low-medium heat for 3 minutes until set but moist.
Serve with toast or hot rotis.

name: Chirer Polao
description: Flattened rice tossed with peanuts, coconut, and a hint of sugar — Bengali breakfast rice in 15 minutes.
steps: Rinse poha briefly in a sieve, drain. Should remain firm, not mushy.
Heat ghee. Fry peanuts golden, remove. Fry coconut pieces briefly.
Add bay leaf, green chillies to the same ghee.
Add poha, sugar, salt. Toss gently 3-4 minutes on low heat.
Fold in peanuts and coconut. Serve warm.

name: Dudh Muri
description: Puffed rice in warm sweetened milk with banana slices — the ultimate no-cook Bengali breakfast, 5 minutes flat.
steps: Warm milk and stir in jaggery until dissolved.
Place muri in a bowl, add banana slices.
Pour hot sweet milk over. Let soak 30 seconds.
Eat immediately — muri should be soft but not mushy.

name: Muri Chanachur
description: Puffed rice tossed with spicy chanachur, onion, mustard oil — the Bengali equivalent of granola, ready in 5 minutes.

ingredients.name: Chanachur (Bengali mixture)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Haldiram or Gopal chanachur/bhujia widely available

steps: In a bowl, combine muri and chanachur.
Add chopped onion and green chilli.
Drizzle mustard oil and lemon juice. Toss well.
Eat immediately — muri goes soft if left.

name: Paratha Dim Roll
description: Frozen paratha + omelette + sliced onion + chilli sauce = 10-minute weekday breakfast roll. Grab and eat on the way out.

ingredients.name: Frozen paratha
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: ID Fresh, Kawan, or Haldiram frozen parathas all work

steps: Cook paratha as per pack until golden.
Beat egg with salt, pepper, chopped chilli. Pour into pan.
Place paratha on top, press gently. Flip after 30 sec.
Add sliced onion and sauce. Roll up tight.
Wrap bottom in foil. Eat immediately.

name: Bengali-Style Poha
description: Poha with a Bengali twist — panch phoron tempering, mustard oil, and green peas. Fragrant, quick, and filling.

ingredients.name: Panch phoron
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Mix cumin + fennel + nigella + fenugreek + mustard seeds

steps: Rinse poha gently and drain.
Heat mustard oil. Add panch phoron; when it crackles, add onion.
When onion softens, add peas and turmeric. Cook 2 minutes.
Add poha, salt, sugar. Toss 3 minutes on low.
Finish with lemon juice. Serve warm.

name: Banana Posto Shake
description: Banana smoothie with a Bengali twist — ground poppy seeds for a nutty, creamy texture. Protein and energy to fuel a Bangalore workday.

ingredients.name: Poppy seed powder (posto)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Toast poppy seeds lightly, then grind; or use 1 tbsp almond butter

steps: Blend banana, milk, posto, honey, and cardamom until smooth.
Taste — adjust sweetness if needed.
Pour into a glass. Drink immediately or pour into a flask.

name: Musoor Dal
description: Bengali red lentil dal — thin, lemony, with a mustard-nigella tempering. The everyday dal that Bengalis eat more days than not.
steps: Pressure cook dal with turmeric, salt, tomato, and 2 cups water for 3 whistles.
Heat mustard oil in a pan. Add kalonji, dried red chillies, slit green chillies.
Pour hot tempering into the cooked dal.
Simmer 2 minutes. Serve with rice and a wedge of lemon.

name: Bhaja Mug Dal
description: Dry-roasted moong dal cooked to a nutty, fragrant gravy with ginger, cumin, and a ghee tempering. The superior Bengali dal.
steps: Dry roast moong dal in a pan on low until golden and fragrant, 3-4 minutes.
Pressure cook with turmeric, ginger, salt and 2.5 cups water for 3 whistles.
Heat ghee. Add bay leaf, cumin, red chilli.
Pour into dal. Add sugar. Simmer 3 minutes.
Serve with rice and a drizzle of raw mustard oil.

name: Cholar Dal
description: Chana dal in sweet-spiced Bengali style with coconut chunks and raisins. The classic puja-bhog dal, ready in 30 minutes.
steps: Pressure cook chana dal with turmeric, salt for 4 whistles.
Fry coconut cubes in ghee until golden. Remove.
Temper with cumin, bay leaf, cardamom, cinnamon.
Add ginger paste, cook 1 min. Pour in cooked dal.
Add coconut, raisins, sugar. Simmer 5 min.
Serve with luchi or rice.

name: Tok Dal
description: A light sour dal made with raw mango or tomato — Bengali comfort on a hot Bangalore day. Cooling, tangy, digestive.

ingredients.name: Raw mango
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Seasonal — use 1 chopped tomato + 1 tbsp amchur when raw mango unavailable

steps: Pressure cook dal with turmeric, salt, raw mango for 3 whistles.
Heat mustard oil. Add mustard seeds and dried red chilli.
Pour tempering into dal. Add sugar.
Simmer 3 minutes. Should be soup-thin and tangy.
Serve with rice; great on a hot day.

name: Labra
description: Mixed vegetable stew tempered with panch phoron — five-spice Bengali vegetable medley. Hearty, healthy, forgiving.

ingredients.name: Panch phoron + bay leaf + dried chilli

steps: Heat mustard oil. Temper with panch phoron, bay leaf, dried chilli.
Add ginger paste. Then add all vegetables.
Add turmeric, salt, sugar. Mix well.
Cover and cook on low 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until vegetables collapse into each other.
Serve with rice or roti.

name: Aloo Bhaja
description: Crispy matchstick-thin fried potatoes with turmeric and salt — the Bengali side that accompanies dal-bhat. Addictive and fast.
steps: Soak julienned potatoes in water 5 min, drain thoroughly, pat dry.
Toss with turmeric and salt.
Heat mustard oil; add green chilli.
Add potatoes. Fry on medium-high, tossing frequently, 8-10 min until crisp.
Drain on paper. Serve with dal-rice.

name: Begun Bhaja
description: Thick eggplant slices pan-fried with turmeric in mustard oil. The side dish that elevates any Bengali meal.
steps: Rub eggplant slices with turmeric, chilli, sugar, and salt. Rest 5 min.
Heat mustard oil in a pan.
Place eggplant slices in single layer. Fry 3 min each side on medium.
Slices should be deeply golden with crisp edges.
Serve with dal-rice.

name: Alur Dom
description: Baby potatoes in a lightly spiced tomato-ginger gravy — Bengali alu-dum in 25 minutes. Great with luchi or puri.
steps: Lightly fry potatoes in mustard oil until golden. Remove.
In same oil, temper with cumin seeds and bay leaf. Add ginger paste.
Add tomato puree + all powders. Cook 4 min until oil separates.
Add potatoes and ½ cup water. Simmer 10 min.
Finish with garam masala. Serve hot.

name: Pepe Chingri
description: Raw papaya and small prawns in a light Bengali gravy — surprising combination, beautifully balanced.
steps: Marinate prawns with turmeric, salt. Lightly fry in mustard oil. Remove.
Temper oil with panch phoron. Add onion paste, cook 5 min.
Add ginger paste, turmeric, chilli. Cook 2 min.
Add papaya, ½ cup water, salt. Cover, cook 10 min.
Add prawns back. Cook 5 more min. Serve with rice.
prepNote: Marinate prawns with turmeric and salt 10 min before cooking.

name: Dim-er Jhol
description: Bengali egg curry with potato cubes in a spiced tomato gravy. Student food, bachelor food, emergency-dinner food. Never fails.
steps: Prick boiled eggs, rub with turmeric + salt. Lightly fry in mustard oil until golden spots. Remove.
In same oil, temper with bay leaf. Fry onion golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste, tomato, all spices. Cook 4 min.
Add potato + 2 cups water + salt. Cover, cook 12 min until potato tender.
Add eggs back. Simmer 3 min. Serve with rice.

name: Kumro Chokka
description: Pumpkin chunks with chickpeas in a warm Bengali masala — sweet-spicy, hearty, perfect weekday veg.

ingredients.name: Panch phoron + bay leaf + dried chilli

steps: Heat mustard oil. Temper with panch phoron, bay leaf, dried chilli.
Add ginger paste, then pumpkin. Toss 3 min.
Add chickpeas, turmeric, chilli, salt, sugar.
Add ½ cup water. Cover, cook 15-18 min until pumpkin is soft and starts to break down.
Serve with roti or rice.

name: Quick Shukto
description: A simplified version of Bengal's bittersweet mixed-veg classic — bitter gourd, eggplant, potato in a milk-paanch-phoron gravy. Eaten first in a Bengali meal as a digestive opener.

ingredients.name: Radhuni or celery seed
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Use celery seed if radhuni unavailable

steps: Lightly fry each vegetable separately in mustard oil. Remove.
Temper ghee with radhuni. Add ginger paste.
Add all fried veggies, panch phoron powder, salt, sugar, and 1 cup water.
Simmer 10 min until softened.
Pour milk in at the end, simmer 2 min (don't boil). Serve first in a meal with hot rice.

name: Chorchori
description: A dry mixed vegetable stir-fry with leafy greens and gourds — whatever is in the fridge, Bengali-spiced. The original zero-waste cooking.
steps: Heat mustard oil. Temper with panch phoron and dried chilli.
Add hard vegetables first (potato, pumpkin). Cook 5 min.
Add softer vegetables, turmeric, salt, green chilli.
Cover, cook 10 min on low until everything is soft.
Uncover, cook 3 min to dry up. Serve with rice.

name: Posto Bora
description: Poppy seed fritters — crispy outside, creamy inside, smoky-nutty flavour. Eat with dal-rice or as a snack.

ingredients.name: Poppy seeds
ingredients.amount: ½ cup, soaked 30 min and ground

steps: Mix poppy paste, onion, green chilli, rice flour, and salt into a thick batter.
Heat mustard oil in a pan.
Drop spoonfuls of batter. Flatten lightly.
Shallow fry 2 min per side until golden and crisp.
Serve hot with rice and dal.

name: Aloo Phulkopi
description: Cauliflower and potato in a light ginger-cumin gravy. Classic winter Bengali veg, but works year-round in Bangalore.

ingredients.name: Ginger paste + cumin powder + turmeric + chilli

steps: Lightly fry cauliflower and potato in mustard oil. Remove.
Temper oil with cumin seeds, bay leaf. Add tomato + ginger.
Add turmeric, chilli, cumin powder, salt. Cook 3 min.
Add veggies + 1 cup water. Cover, cook 12 min.
Finish with a pinch of garam masala. Serve with rice or roti.

name: Lau Chingri
description: Bottle gourd with small prawns — surprisingly addictive sweet-savoury dish. Light, healthy, and ready in 30 minutes.

ingredients.name: Mustard oil + bay leaf + cumin seeds

steps: Lightly fry prawns in mustard oil. Remove.
Temper oil with bay leaf, cumin. Add onion paste, cook 4 min.
Add ginger, turmeric, chilli. Add lau cubes, salt.
Cover, cook 15 min — bottle gourd releases water, no extra needed.
Add prawns, simmer 5 min. Serve with rice.

name: Tomato Khejur Chutney
description: Sweet tomato chutney with dates and raisins — served at the end of a Bengali meal. Tangy, jammy, soul-satisfying.
steps: Heat mustard oil. Temper with panch phoron and dried chilli.
Add tomatoes, a pinch of salt, turmeric. Cook 5 min until pulpy.
Add dates, raisins, sugar. Cook on low 10 min until jammy.
Finish with roasted panch phoron powder.
Cool. Serve at end of the meal with a papor.

name: Brick Lane Chicken Bhuna
description: London Bangladeshi curry-house style bhuna — dry, deeply spiced chicken with caramelised onions. Brick Lane's weekend staple.

ingredients.name: Curry-house base gravy powder
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Use 1 tsp cumin + 1 tsp coriander + ½ tsp fenugreek powder

steps: Fry onions in oil until deeply golden (~12 min). Remove half, keep rest in pan.
Add ginger-garlic paste + all dry spices. Cook 2 min.
Add tomato paste, cook until oil separates.
Add chicken, sear 4 min. Add fried onions back + salt + ½ cup water.
Cook uncovered on medium 20 min until sauce clings thickly to chicken.
Finish with garam masala. Serve with naan or rice.

name: NYC Bengali Grain Bowl
description: Quinoa or brown rice topped with turmeric-spiced chicken, raita, pickled onions, and a kasundi drizzle. Bengali wellness bowl.
steps: Marinate chicken with spices. Pan-sear 12-15 min until caramelised.
Quick-pickle onion in lemon juice + pinch of salt, 10 min.
Whisk yoghurt with roasted cumin + salt = raita.
Assemble bowl: grain base, chicken, raita, pickled onion, coriander.
Drizzle kasundi over. Done.
prepNote: Marinate chicken 20 min ahead; quick-pickle the onion 10 min before serving.

name: Sydney Bengali Prawn Salad
description: Sydney-style warm prawn salad with raw mango, coriander, and a mustard-honey dressing. Bright, light, Bangalore-summer perfect.

ingredients.name: Raw mango
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Seasonal; use green apple out of season

ingredients.name: Turmeric + chilli + salt
ingredients.amount: ¼ tsp + ¼ tsp + to taste

steps: Marinate prawns with turmeric, chilli, salt. Pan-sear in olive oil 4 min.
Whisk kasundi, honey, lime, olive oil into a dressing.
Toss greens, cucumber, tomato, raw mango, coriander in a bowl.
Top with warm prawns. Drizzle dressing. Serve immediately.
prepNote: Marinate prawns 10 min before searing; the dressing can be made a day ahead.

name: Toronto Nolen Gur Cookies
description: Chocolate chip cookies with maple syrup and nolen gur — a Toronto Bengali's contribution to world baking. Chewy, deep-caramel.

ingredients.name: Nolen gur
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Regular jaggery + ½ tsp molasses

steps: Beat butter with nolen gur and maple syrup until creamy.
Beat in egg. Fold in flour, baking soda, salt.
Fold in chocolate chips.
Scoop onto baking tray. Bake at 180°C for 12-14 min until edges are golden.
Cool 5 min before eating.

name: Dubai Bengali Mixed Grill Roll
description: Dubai-style mixed grill (chicken tikka + seekh) wrapped in a paratha with garlic sauce. The Gulf Bengali taxi driver's favourite roll.
steps: Marinate chicken cubes with half tandoori + half yoghurt + ginger-garlic, 20 min.
Mix minced meat with remaining tandoori, form small sausages.
Grill or pan-sear both until cooked.
Cook parathas. Layer garlic-yoghurt sauce + chicken + seekh + onion + tomato + pickled chilli.
Roll tight in foil. Eat hot.
prepNote: Marinate chicken and mince for 20 min before grilling.

name: Bangladeshi-British Chicken Tikka Masala
description: The UK's national dish — invented by Bangladeshi chefs in Glasgow/Birmingham. Creamy tomato, grilled chicken, deep warmth.

ingredients.name: Kashmiri chilli + cumin + coriander + garam masala

steps: Marinate chicken in yoghurt + ginger-garlic + tandoori masala for 30 min. Grill or pan-char.
In oil + butter, cook onion puree 6 min. Add spices, cook 2 min.
Add tomato puree, salt, sugar. Simmer 10 min till thick.
Add grilled chicken, cream, kasuri methi. Simmer 8 min.
Serve with naan or basmati rice.
prepNote: Marinate chicken in yoghurt and spices for at least 30 min (overnight is better).

name: Singapore Mishti Pulao
description: Singapore Bengali community's version of mishti pulao — saffron rice with pandan leaves, cashews, and raisins. A diaspora mash-up.

ingredients.name: Pandan leaf
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Skip or substitute 2 bay leaves

steps: Soak rice 20 min, drain.
Fry cashews and raisins in ghee. Remove.
Add whole garam masala, bay leaf, pandan to the ghee.
Add rice, salt, sugar, and 2¾ cups water + saffron milk.
Cover, cook on low 15 min. Fluff and fold in cashews + raisins.
Serve warm — great alongside chicken rezala or kosha mangsho.
prepNote: Soak rice for 20 min before cooking so it cooks evenly and stays fluffy.

name: NYC Bengali Breakfast Burrito
description: NYC food-cart Bengali's weekday breakfast — tortilla wrap with spiced scrambled eggs, potato bhaji, and kasundi. Protein-packed grab-and-go.
steps: Mash potato with turmeric, chilli, salt.
Scramble eggs in oil with onion and green chilli.
Warm tortilla. Spread potato mash + scrambled egg.
Top with coriander + kasundi.
Roll tight, halve, eat on the way out.

name: Melbourne Coconut Fish Curry
description: Melbourne-style light fish curry with coconut milk and Bengali five-spice — what Aussie-Bengali families cook on Tuesdays.
steps: Marinate fish with turmeric, chilli, salt.
Heat oil. Temper with panch phoron and curry leaves.
Add ginger paste, cook 2 min. Add coconut milk + salt.
Bring to gentle simmer. Slide in fish.
Simmer 10 min until fish is just cooked.
Finish with lemon juice. Serve over basmati.
prepNote: Marinate fish with turmeric, chilli, and salt 15 min before cooking.

name: London Bengali Roast Chicken
description: A whole chicken roast with turmeric-mustard-garlic butter under the skin — the UK Bengali Sunday dinner fusion that changes everything.
steps: Mix butter with mustard, turmeric, chilli, garam masala, garlic.
Loosen chicken skin. Rub spiced butter under and over the skin.
Stuff cavity with half lemon. Rub outside with salt + pepper.
Lay chopped onion, potato, carrot in a roasting tray, place chicken on top.
Roast at 200°C for 60-70 min, basting halfway, until juices run clear.
Rest 10 min. Carve. Spoon pan juices over.

name: Kosha Mangsho
description: Slow-cooked Bengali mutton in a deep, dark, onion-based gravy — the ritual Sunday lunch of Kolkata. No shortcuts.
steps: Marinate mutton in yoghurt + ginger-garlic + salt, 1 hour.
Heat oil. Add sugar, let caramelize. Add onions, fry 15 min till deep brown.
Add whole garam masala + bay leaf. Add mutton + potatoes.
Add all powdered spices. Cook on medium-high, stirring, for 15 min — this 'kosha' step is crucial.
Add hot water to cover. Pressure cook 25 min, or slow simmer 50 min.
Finish with a spoonful of ghee. Rest 10 min. Serve with luchi or rice.
prepNote: Marinate mutton in yoghurt and ginger-garlic for at least 1 hour — overnight in the fridge gives deeper flavour.

name: Bhetki Paturi
description: Bhetki fish fillets smeared with mustard-coconut paste, wrapped in banana leaves, steamed. Pure Bengali umami.

ingredients.name: Bhetki fillets
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Seer (surmai) or basa fillets work; bhetki occasionally at big fish markets

ingredients.name: Banana leaves
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Baking parchment + foil wrap works; banana leaves at Gandhi Bazaar

steps: Soak mustard seeds 20 min. Grind with coconut, green chillies, salt, turmeric into thick paste.
Marinate fish pieces in this paste + 1 tbsp mustard oil for 20 min.
Lay fish on softened banana leaf, add a green chilli on top, drizzle mustard oil.
Fold leaf into a parcel. Tie with string or toothpick.
Steam for 15 min, or pan-steam in a lidded skillet for 12 min.
Open at table. Serve with hot rice.
prepNote: Soak mustard seeds 20 min, grind paste, then marinate fish in the paste for a further 20 min.

name: Chingri Bhapa
description: Prawns steamed in a mustard-coconut-yoghurt paste. No frying, maximum flavour. Bengali purity at its peak.

ingredients.name: Turmeric + salt + sugar
ingredients.amount: ½ tsp + to taste + ½ tsp

steps: Soak mustard seeds 20 min. Grind smooth with coconut, chillies, yoghurt, turmeric, salt, sugar.
Combine prawns with the paste + 2 tbsp mustard oil.
Transfer to a steel tiffin box or heatproof bowl with a lid. Drizzle remaining mustard oil and place 2 green chillies on top.
Steam 20-25 min in a pressure cooker (no whistle) or steamer.
Rest 5 min before opening. Serve with hot rice.

name: Katla Kalia
description: Katla fish in a rich, reddish onion-tomato gravy — reserved for Bengali feasts and Sunday lunches. Royal but achievable.

ingredients.name: Kashmiri chilli + cumin + coriander + garam masala
Mustard oil + bay leaf + whole garam masala

steps: Marinate fish with turmeric + salt. Fry in mustard oil until golden both sides. Remove.
In same oil, temper with bay leaf + whole garam masala.
Add onion paste, cook 8 min till golden.
Add ginger-garlic paste, spices. Cook 3 min.
Add tomato + yoghurt + sugar + salt. Cook 8 min till oil separates.
Add 1 cup water and fish. Simmer 10 min. Finish with garam masala. Serve with rice or pulao.
prepNote: Marinate fish with turmeric and salt 10 min before frying.

name: Pabda Jhol
description: Pabda fish in a delicate turmeric-nigella broth with green chillies. Light enough for a weekday, elegant enough for guests.

ingredients.name: Pabda fish
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Try frozen pabda at BigBasket; pomfret baby or tilapia as substitute

steps: Marinate fish with turmeric + salt.
Heat mustard oil. Shallow fry fish very gently 1 min per side. Remove.
Add kalonji + green chillies to oil. Add tomato + ginger paste. Cook 3 min.
Add 1.5 cups water + turmeric + salt. Boil 2 min.
Slide fish in. Simmer 8 min on low.
Drizzle raw mustard oil. Serve with rice.
prepNote: Marinate fish with turmeric and salt 10 min ahead — they are delicate, so handle gently.

name: Basanti Pulao
description: Bengali sweet saffron pulao with cashews and raisins. The pulao of weddings, Durga puja, and Sunday specials.

ingredients.name: Gobindobhog rice
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Short-grain fragrant rice works; gobindobhog on BigBasket

steps: Wash rice gently, drain on cloth 30 min. Rub 2 tbsp ghee + turmeric + salt + sugar into rice.
Heat remaining ghee. Fry cashews and raisins, remove.
Add bay leaves and whole garam masala.
Add rice, stir gently 2 min.
Add 2¾ cups water + saffron milk. Cover, cook on low 15 min.
Turn off heat, rest 10 min. Fold in cashews + raisins. Serve with chicken rezala or kosha mangsho.

name: Rasgulla
description: Soft, spongy chhena balls simmered in sugar syrup — Bengal's most famous sweet, achievable at home with patience.
steps: Boil milk. Off heat, add lemon juice slowly till milk curdles.
Strain through muslin, rinse under cold water to remove tartness. Hang 15 min.
Knead chhena on a plate for 8 minutes until smooth and greasy — this is critical.
Roll into 12-14 small smooth balls, no cracks.
Boil sugar + water + cardamom. Drop balls in. Cover, boil vigorously 15 min — they double in size.
Cool completely in syrup. Refrigerate 3+ hours before serving.
prepNote: Make chhena 30 min before starting: curdle milk with lemon, strain through muslin, rinse, and hang for 15 min to drain.

name: Sandesh
description: Fresh chhena sweet with cardamom — dry, melt-in-mouth, elegant. The fancy Bengali sweet box staple.
steps: Make chhena (see rasgulla steps). Hang only 20 min — don't over-dry.
Knead chhena on a plate 10 min until creamy.
Transfer to pan with sugar + cardamom. Cook on low 6-8 min, stirring constantly, until thickens.
Off heat, cool slightly. Shape into small discs or press into moulds.
Garnish with pistachio. Cool 30 min. Serve chilled.
prepNote: Make chhena 30 min before starting: curdle milk, strain through muslin, rinse, and hang 20 min. Don't over-drain.

name: Chhanar Payesh
description: Reduced sweet milk studded with tiny chhena pearls — richer than rice kheer, lighter than rabdi.
steps: Curdle 500ml milk with lemon. Strain, rinse, hang 15 min.
Knead chhena 5 min. Roll into tiny pea-sized balls.
In separate pan, reduce 750ml milk with cardamom + bay leaf + saffron till half, stirring often.
Add sugar to reduced milk. Cook 2 min.
Gently drop chhena balls in. Simmer 8 min — don't stir roughly.
Cool. Refrigerate 3 hours. Garnish with nuts.
prepNote: Make chhena 30 min before starting: curdle 500 ml milk with lemon, strain, rinse, and hang 15 min in muslin.

name: Pantua
description: Bengali gulab jamun — deep-fried chhena + khoya balls soaked in sugar syrup. Darker, denser, more indulgent than its northern cousin.
steps: Knead chhena + khoya + maida + semolina + cardamom + soda into smooth dough.
Roll into smooth balls, no cracks.
Heat ghee to medium-low. Fry balls slowly, turning often, 8-10 min until deep brown.
Prepare thin sugar syrup separately (one-string consistency).
Drop fried balls into warm syrup. Let soak 2+ hours.
Serve warm or room temperature.
prepNote: Make chhena 30 min before starting: curdle milk with lemon, strain, rinse, and hang 15 min in muslin, then knead.

name: Chomchom
description: Oval Bangladeshi milk sweet from Tangail — chewier and denser than rasgulla, often layered with cardamom mawa on top.
steps: Make chhena (curdle milk with lemon, strain, rinse, hang 15 min).
Knead chhena 8 min till smooth.
Roll into oblong shapes, about 1.5 inches long.
Boil sugar + water + cardamom.
Drop chomchoms in. Cover, boil 20 min — they swell and deepen to honey colour.
Cool in syrup. Optionally top with crumbled mawa. Refrigerate before serving.
prepNote: Make chhena 30 min before starting: curdle milk with lemon, strain, rinse, and hang 15 min before kneading.

name: Ledikeni
description: Cylinder-shaped fried chhena sweet created in 1856 for Lady Canning — deep caramel outside, soft syrup-soaked inside.
steps: Mix chhena, khoya, maida, soda, cardamom. Knead into smooth dough.
Shape into small oval cylinders, 1.5 inch.
Heat ghee to low-medium. Fry gently 10 min, turning, until deep reddish-brown all over.
Make thin syrup in separate pan (3 cups water + 1.5 cups sugar + cardamom).
Drop fried ledikenis into hot syrup. Soak 3-4 hours till saturated.
Serve at room temperature.
prepNote: Make chhena 30 min before starting: curdle milk with lemon, strain, rinse, and hang 15 min before mixing the dough.

name: Masoor Dal-er Dum
description: Everyday red lentils finished dum-style with a kalo jeere + kancha lonka tadka. The 10-minute rest after tempering is where the magic happens — flavour doubles.

ingredients.name: Kalo jeere (nigella seeds)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Nature's Basket or MTR stocks it; else substitute with cumin seeds

steps: Rinse masoor dal. Pressure cook with tomato, turmeric, salt, green chilli and 3 cups water — 3 whistles (~8 min).
Whisk the cooked dal till smooth. Simmer while you make the tadka.
Heat mustard oil till smoking, cool slightly. Crackle kalo jeere + dried red chilli.
Pour tadka over dal. Immediately cover with a tight lid — this is the dum step.
Let sit, heat off, for 10 min. The nigella flavour infuses beautifully.
Finish with ghee and coriander. Serve with rice.

name: Kolkata Biryani
description: The Awadhi-Bengali hybrid — fragrant biryani where the potato is as important as the meat. No food colouring, no overthinking. Just kewra, saffron, and patience.

ingredients.name: Basmati rice (aged, long-grain)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Aged Basmati from Domlur SPAR or Namdhari's. Avoid supermarket house brands — the grain length matters here.

ingredients.name: Mutton (bone-in, shoulder cut)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Halal mutton from Shivajinagar market or Fresh-to-Home delivery. Ask for shoulder — it holds up to slow cooking.

ingredients.name: Kewra water
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Nature's Basket or Iyengar Bakery area shops stock Ruh Kewra. Don't skip — it's what makes Kolkata biryani smell like Kolkata biryani.

ingredients.name: Saffron
ingredients.amount: a generous pinch, soaked in warm milk
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Namdhari's or Nuts n Spices on Residency Road. Buy Kashmiri — Iranian saffron at those prices is usually fake.

ingredients.name: Whole spices (bay leaf, cloves, cardamom, cinnamon, star anise)

steps: Marinate mutton in yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, fried onion, whole spices, salt, and a pinch of saffron milk. At least 2 hours, overnight is better.
Parboil basmati in heavily salted water with whole spices until 70% cooked. Drain immediately.
In a heavy-bottomed pot, layer: mutton at the bottom, rice in the middle, fried potatoes tucked in.
Drizzle kewra water, rose water, saffron milk, and ghee over the top layer of rice.
Seal with a tight lid or dough. Cook dum on low heat for 35–40 min.
Rest for 10 minutes before opening. Mix gently from the bottom when serving.
prepNote: Marinate mutton overnight if possible. Fried onions (beresta) can be made a day ahead.

name: Dhokar Dalna
description: Fried lentil cakes in a spiced potato gravy. The vegetarian showstopper at any Bengali thali — takes effort, rewards generously.

ingredients.name: Coconut, fresh grated
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Fresh coconut from any Bangalore vegetable vendor or KR Market. Frozen grated coconut from BigBasket also works.

ingredients.name: Asafoetida (hing)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Compounded hing widely available everywhere. For the real Kashmiri hing (resin form), try Sadananda stores in Malleshwaram.

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Nature's Basket, Namdhari's, or Amazon Fresh deliver Kachi Ghani mustard oil.

ingredients.name: Cumin, bay leaf, dried red chilli

steps: Grind soaked chana dal coarsely — no water. Mix with grated coconut, hing, salt, and a pinch of turmeric.
Heat a greased pan and press dal mixture into a 1cm-thick slab. Cook covered on low for 10 min, flip, cook another 10 min.
Cool, cut into diamond shapes. Shallow fry in mustard oil until golden on both sides. Set aside.
In the same pan, temper with cumin seeds, bay leaf, dried chilli. Add potato wedges, fry 3 min.
Add tomato puree, turmeric, cumin and coriander powder. Cook masala until oil separates.
Add 1.5 cups water, bring to boil, cook potatoes until soft.
Gently add fried dhoka pieces. Simmer 5 min — don't over-stir or they break.
Finish with ghee and garam masala. Serve with luchi or rice.
prepNote: Soak chana dal 4 hours minimum. The dal-cake slab can be made a day ahead and refrigerated.

name: Koraishutir Kochuri
description: Kolkata's winter morning in edible form — flaky fried bread stuffed with spiced green pea paste. Peak season is November to February when fresh peas arrive.

ingredients.name: Fresh green peas (shelled)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Fresh peas available at Russell Market and Jayanagar 4th Block vegetable market Nov–Feb. Off-season: frozen peas from BigBasket work almost as well — thaw and pat dry first.

steps: Blanch peas 2 minutes, drain. Coarsely grind with ginger and chilli — keep it textured, not a paste.
Temper oil with cumin and hing. Add pea mixture, salt, a pinch of sugar. Cook stirring until dry, about 8 min. Cool completely.
Knead maida with a pinch of salt, 2 tsp oil, and warm water into a firm dough. Rest 20 min.
Divide dough into golf-ball portions. Flatten, place a tbsp of filling in centre, seal tightly.
Roll gently into 10cm circles. Don't press too hard or filling escapes.
Deep fry on medium heat, pressing down gently so they puff. Fry until pale golden — about 2 min per side.
Drain and serve immediately with aloo dum or tomato khejur chutney.
prepNote: Filling must be completely cool before stuffing or the dough tears. Make filling a day ahead if needed.

name: Macher Matha Diye Dal
description: Moong dal cooked with a fried fish head — the fish head melts into the dal and gives it a depth you can't fake. Bengali home cooking at its most honest.

ingredients.name: Rohu or Katla fish head
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Rohu and Katla heads from KR Market fish section or Domlur fish market. Ask the vendor to split it for you. ₹30–50 per head. Fresh-to-Home also delivers cleaned fish heads.

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Kachi Ghani mustard oil from Nature's Basket, Namdhari's, or Amazon Fresh.

ingredients.name: Kalo jeere (nigella seeds)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Nature's Basket stocks it. Substitute with cumin seeds if unavailable — flavour changes but it works.

steps: Dry roast moong dal in a pan until lightly golden and fragrant. Boil with turmeric and salt until soft.
Marinate fish head with turmeric and salt.
Heat mustard oil until smoking. Fry fish head pieces until brown and crisp on all sides. Remove and set aside.
In the same oil, crackle kalo jeere. Add ginger paste and green chillies. Cook 1 minute.
Add cooked dal, stir to combine. Add water if too thick — consistency should be slightly runny.
Add fried fish head pieces. Simmer covered on low heat 10 minutes.
The fish head will soften and its fat will enrich the dal. Stir gently before serving.
Serve with steamed rice. This dal improves as it sits.
prepNote: Dry roasting the moong dal before boiling is the key step — adds a nuttiness that makes this dish.

name: Kanchkolar Kofta
description: Raw banana koftas in a ginger-tomato gravy — this is the Bengali answer to meat-free weekdays. The koftas are earthy and filling in a way that makes you forget there's no protein.

ingredients.name: Raw banana (kanchkola / kacha kela)
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Raw bananas are widely available at Bangalore vegetable markets — no substitute needed. Look for firm, completely green ones at Jayanagar 4th Block or your local sabziwala. Avoid any with yellow tinge.

ingredients.name: Mustard oil
ingredients.amount: for frying koftas + 2 tbsp for gravy
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Kachi Ghani mustard oil from Nature's Basket or Namdhari's.

ingredients.name: Fresh coconut, grated
ingredients.bangaloreSubstitute: Fresh coconut from any Bangalore vegetable vendor. Frozen grated coconut from BigBasket also works well.

steps: Boil raw bananas with skin in salted water until tender (~15 min). Peel while warm and mash well.
Mix mashed banana with mashed potato, grated coconut, ginger, salt, a pinch of garam masala, and a little sugar.
Shape into smooth oval koftas. If mix is sticky, wet your hands.
Deep fry koftas in mustard oil on medium heat until golden brown all over. Drain and set aside.
For gravy: temper oil with cumin and bay leaf. Add ginger paste, cook 1 minute.
Add tomato puree, turmeric, cumin powder. Cook until oil separates, about 8 min.
Add 1 cup water, salt, pinch of sugar. Bring to a simmer.
Add koftas just before serving — don't boil them in the gravy or they break. Just a 2-minute warm through.
Finish with garam masala and serve with rice or luchi.
prepNote: Koftas can be fried ahead and stored. Add to gravy only at serving time.
